data.title: Despite 40% drop in fee income, pump.fun still earned $247 million in less than 2 months
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of on-chain analyst Yu Jin, pump.fun transferred 65,000 SOL (US$11.01 million) to Kraken 5 hours ago.
Due to the diversion of BSC funds/attention and the influence of the "conspiracy group" of LIBRA's coin issuance, people's PvP transactions on pump have decreased, so the handling fees earned by pump are about 40% less than before, so the number of coins sold this time is also less.
However, less than 2 months have passed in 2025, and pump has sold 1.235 million SOL (US$247.45 million). Of course, another way to say it is: in less than two months, pump has earned US$247 million.

data.title: Nic Carter: The era of Memecoin as a fair trading opportunity is over, regulators may take action against insider trading
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Castle Island Ventures partner Nic Carter said that the era of meme coins as a fair trading opportunity is "undoubtedly over."
Carter believes that meme coins (tokens with little use other than speculative trading) were initially attractive because they seemed to provide a level playing field for retail investors. However, with the recent scandals such as the LIBRA coin, the market has been taken over by insiders, pre-launch trading, and robot-driven exchanges, putting everyday traders at a disadvantage.
He wrote: "The whole premise of meme coins is that they provide a 'fair launch' opportunity, and retail investors have the same opportunities as funds and VCs. This has been exposed as a lie-the casino is not fair."
While Carter believes that the recent trading frenzy has ended since US President Trump launched TRUMP, he did point out that the industry will not disappear. On the contrary, there may still be some new token issuances and some winners, but "the meta era is over."
As confidence in Meme Coin fades, Carter expects regulators to take action against insider trading in the industry, "Just because Meme Coin may not be a security, it doesn't mean that insider trading is not liable," predicting that blockchain transaction history will lead to future enforcement actions.
Looking forward, Carter believes the market will move toward more sustainable and fairer token issuance. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Aptos research director accused Monad of plagiarism, Monad co-founder denied it and said "I was developing it when you were still in diapers"
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Aptos Research Director Alexander Spiegelman wrote in X that he didn't understand why Monad spent so much time plagiarizing Aptos' technology. All of this is open source, and there are already many peer-reviewed papers. Instead of hiding it, it's better to plagiarize it directly.
In response, Monad co-founder James Hunsaker said: "When you were still wearing diapers, I was already studying software transactional memory (STM) in the Haskell environment. BlockSTM is a simple extension of these things. I have never seen any Aptos code. In fact, if you hadn't published this nonsense, I wouldn't have thought of Aptos."

data.title: SEC Withdraws Appeal Against Ruling on DeFi Regulation
data.description:
According to Odaily, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has voluntarily withdrawn its appeal against a ruling that prevented the agency from expanding existing securities laws to include decentralized finance (DeFi) users and projects. The SEC submitted a four-page motion to the U.S. Fifth Circuit Court of Appeals expressing its intention to withdraw the appeal, which faced no opposition.

In November of last year, a federal judge in Texas ruled that the SEC's attempt to broaden the legal definition of 'dealers' exceeded its authority. The judge found that the changes to the SEC's dealer rules were unlawful as they conflated DeFi traders with financial brokers.

Kristin Smith, CEO of the Blockchain Association, stated that the SEC's decision to withdraw the appeal marks a significant victory for the digital asset industry. She emphasized that the initial lawsuit against the SEC was filed to challenge the agency's unauthorized attempt to redefine its statutory powers. Smith expressed hope for productive dialogue between the industry and the SEC following the dismissal under the agency's new leadership.

data.title: Cardex Confirms Security Breach Linked To Session Key Exposure
data.description: According to Odaily, the Cardex ecosystem application recently experienced a security breach. Following an initial security audit, the team inadvertently exposed the private key of the session signer on their website's front end. Cardex has confirmed on the X platform that the attack is related to the session key leak. The team is currently collaborating with Abstract to trace the stolen funds and assess the recovery process. Further details about the incident will be disclosed in due course.

data.title: Cardex Protocol Faces Attack, Efforts Underway For Fund Recovery
data.description: According to BlockBeats, on February 20, the Abstract ecosystem project Cardex announced that its protocol was attacked on February 18. The team is currently collaborating with the Abstract team to trace the flow of funds and work towards their recovery.

data.title: pump.fun has deposited 2,345,499 SOL to Kraken so far, equivalent to $473 million
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to Lookonchain monitoring, pump.fun has deposited 2,345,499 SOL (US$473 million) to Kraken so far, and sold 264,373 SOL to obtain 41.64 million USDC.
pump.fun has earned a total of 2.93 million SOL (US$515 million).

data.title: User Loses Over $68,000 in Transfer Record Pollution Attack
data.description: According to Foresight News, Scam Sniffer has detected a transfer record pollution attack that resulted in a user losing more than $68,000. The attacker employed a tactic involving the sending of fake transfers with similar addresses, causing the fraudulent address to appear in the user's transaction history. This led the user to mistakenly transfer funds to the attacker's address.

data.title: Cardex: Working with Abstract team to track stolen funds
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Abstract previously disclosed that the ecological application Cardex suffered a security vulnerability attack. After completing the initial security audit, its team accidentally exposed the private key of the session signer on the front end of its website. In response, Cardex issued a statement on the X platform confirming that it had been attacked related to the leakage of session keys. It is currently working with the Abstract team to track the flow and recovery of the stolen funds, and will disclose the details of the incident later.

data.title: SEC voluntarily withdraws appeal of DeFi rulemaking
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News The U.S. SEC voluntarily withdrew its appeal of a ruling on Wednesday that prevented the agency from expanding the scope of existing securities laws to cover DeFi users and projects.
The SEC expressed its desire to "voluntarily withdraw this appeal" in a four-page motion filed with the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fifth Circuit, and the move was not opposed.
Last November, a federal judge in Texas ruled that the SEC's expansion of the legal definition of "dealer" exceeded the regulator's authority. The judge believed that the amendment to the SEC's dealer rules was illegal because it conflated DeFi traders with financial brokers.
Kristin Smith, CEO of the Blockchain Association, said in a statement that the SEC's voluntary withdrawal of the appeal was a major victory for the digital asset industry. "We first filed a lawsuit against the SEC to challenge the agency's illegal power grab, which attempted to unilaterally redefine the boundaries of its statutory powers. With the agency's new leadership leading to today's final dismissal, we look forward to a productive dialogue between the industry and the SEC." (Decrypt)
